# Error Handling Checklist: Troubleshooting, Security, Trust, Update, And Rollback

**Purpose**: Validate DOC-008 requirements quality for symptom-driven failure diagnosis, safe recovery sequencing, stale-install rollback boundaries, and separation of read-only inspection from mutating repair steps.
**Created**: 2026-06-18
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are required failure classes covered for marketplace source, path confusion, installed cache/runtime state, version drift, permissions/approvals, CLI prerequisites, and Codex custom-agent registration? [Completeness, Spec FR-004, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK002 Does every troubleshooting row requirement include symptom, likely cause, read-only diagnostic command or file, recommended fix, platform label, and follow-up link? [Completeness, Spec FR-003, Spec SC-002]
- [x] CHK003 Are CLI prerequisite failures explicit for missing or outdated Spec Kit CLI, GitHub CLI, and jq rather than hidden under a generic install failure row? [Completeness, Spec FR-004]
- [x] CHK004 Are update, refresh, reinstall, remove, rollback, stale payload, stale cache, and version-sync recovery cases all required with checkpoint, manual action, side effect, reload or restart need, and source citation? [Completeness, Spec FR-011, Spec SC-006]
- [x] CHK005 Are Codex custom-agent registration failures handled separately from plugin installation, bundled skill loading, marketplace operations, and Codex restart? [Completeness, Spec FR-015]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Are read-only inspection cells constrained to state-reporting commands, platform detail views, manual file paths, or source/reference links only? [Clarity, Spec FR-006, Spec Key Entities]
- [x] CHK007 Does the spec explicitly exclude login, install, remove, reload, restart, approve, edit, set, unset, delete, rebuild, config-write, cache-edit, and token/secret-printing commands from inspect cells? [Clarity, Spec FR-006]
- [x] CHK008 Are mutating repair steps allowed only in recommended fixes or linked recovery guidance, with side effects stated before any command or path? [Clarity, Spec FR-006, Spec Manual Recovery Action]
- [x] CHK009 Is stale cache guidance clear that direct cache edits, cache deletion, or cache directory removal are not default fixes and are last-resort manual actions only after safer recovery checks? [Clarity, Spec FR-013, Spec FR-014]
- [x] CHK010 Are marketplace source, generated payload, installed cache/runtime state, copied custom-agent files, and source-vs-generated-payload mismatch kept distinct enough to diagnose stale behavior without guessing? [Clarity, Spec FR-012, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK011 Are rollback anchors defined as known marketplace source, Git ref, generated payload path, manifest version, CLI JSON field, or source-vs-dist evidence rather than vague "go back" language? [Clarity, Spec Rollback Anchor, Spec FR-011]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK012 Do troubleshooting requirements and update/rollback requirements preserve the same safe recovery order: inspect marketplace/source, inspect generated or copied payload state, use platform refresh/reinstall/remove/reload/restart, then Codex custom-agent reinstall if applicable, before last-resort cache guidance? [Consistency, Spec FR-011, Spec FR-012, Plan Documentation Boundaries]
- [x] CHK013 Does Claude Code recovery consistently separate marketplace update/remove, plugin install/uninstall, `/reload-plugins`, plugin detail inspection, managed policy, installed runtime state, and cache behavior? [Consistency, Spec FR-016]
- [x] CHK014 Does Codex recovery consistently separate plugin installation, bundled skill loading, marketplace add/remove/list/upgrade, custom-agent registration through `@SpecKit Pro -> install` or `$install`, and Codex restart? [Consistency, Spec FR-015]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Are row completeness and required failure-class coverage measurable as 100% troubleshooting row checks rather than subjective "clear guidance" language? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-002]
- [x] CHK016 Are recovery outcomes measurable by requiring every recovery case to include checkpoint, manual action, side effect, reload-or-restart expectation, and source evidence? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-006]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK017 Are managed policy and organization-control failures treated as possible causes without telling users to bypass policy, permissions, hooks, MCP, network, or marketplace restrictions? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases, Spec FR-012]
- [x] CHK018 Are browser-rendered docs explicitly prevented from running local diagnostics, granting permissions, invoking workflows, inspecting local filesystems, or automatically repairing plugin state? [Coverage, Spec FR-006]
